name: 高菁晗
topic: Do light and catenary exhibit the least action principle according to the same core concept in nature?
availabilityNote: 因考试原因，下次SA会议为5月29日，在那之前还有4小时1对1理论课要完成

personalEntries.title: 关于思维导图
personalEntries.content: 网站做了个.md转思维导图的程序，但是她那个**太大了**我就不往上放了，查看本地文件吧

sessions.homework:
Hi Eileen,

Once again, great work on the mindmap, a lot of thoughtful ideas.

I have made some annotations on your ideas. These are the legends:

✅: Very promising to develop into an EPQ project 
⭕: Certain potential in developing into an EPQ project
❌: Too “simple” or unrealistic with current scope 
(e.g. experiment limitations)

Could you have a look at the annotated mindmap, and pick three topics (could be single ones or a combination of a couple similar ones) that you find interest you the most, and we will focus on those in our meeting today.

"❌" doesn't necessarily mean "hard no", but instead you will need to spend longer time in developing these into fully grown EPQ topics. If there is anything in this category that strongly interests you, you can still include them in your "top 3", and we will see what we can do.

Things to do next
1. Complete form 2 based on our meeting
2. Continue with form 6
3. Find real-life applications of the two research interests of yours (i.e., light rays & catenary), and look up relevant literatures
4. Look up on the mathematical side (e.g. the Euler-Lagrange Equation) of your research, and make some notes.
5. Start collecting literature and construct a reading log, making sure to use the CRAAP system.

第一第二章：EPQ学习机制+评分标准
sessions.homework:
1. 查看学生用书以及EPQ范文，提前熟悉教材
2. 制作自己的课程表，包括自己的EPQ课程安排，学校学习安排，竞赛安排，等。保持课程表更新
3. 登录并查看自己的EPQ在线系统：https://epqforms.seedasdan.com，熟悉操作
(Optional)开始构思自己的EPQ选题，可以从思考关键词开始

对任何知识点有问题可以随时咨询老师~周末愉快！

第三章 -- 选题标准与技巧

Taught element #3 
第四章：项目管理
sessions.homework:
After-class assignments:
1. Create mindmaps (as many as you want/can!) to formulate your research ideas from personal interests/academic pursuits/past experiences/etc.. Post your mindmaps here and so we can discuss them further in our first meeting.
2. Explore Gantt Chart and start managing your time: evaluate when would be ideal for the remaining hours of your EPQ taught elements (~8.5 hours remaining)

sessions.summary:
Taught element #4
第五章：研究方法与方法论、文献阅读与分析
sessions.homework:
Today's taught element summary & afterclass-assignments:

1. Try drafting your own methodology based on your current research topic
2. Read 3-5 literatures and log it on your reading log, and use credibility tests such as CARRP, PANDA or CRAVEN (or any other valid credibility judgemental frameworks) to analyse them.

接下来该讲方差检验了

Taught Element #6 
第六章：数据分析与图表制作 (2/2)

sessions.title: SA #3
sessions.summary: This meeting focused on reviewing Eileen's progress on her EPQ (Extended Project Qualification) research project and planning next steps. Simon and Eileen discussed her completed work, including Form 2 and a reading log containing textbooks, videos, and online resources. Simon provided guidance on improving the reading list by suggesting the inclusion of peer-reviewed academic papers, particularly recommending a specific article on "Capillary Functions Meet Electromagnetic Waves." They explored the possibility of expanding Eileen's research to include the brachistochrone problem and Fermat's principle, with Simon explaining that these changes could still be made before Form 5 is completed. The conversation ended with instructions for Eileen to fill out Form 4, which includes aims, objectives, activities, resources, and contingency planning, as well as starting work on the dissertation introduction section, which should be approximately 500 words.
sessions.homework:
- Read the academic article on "Capillary Functions Meet Electromagnetic Waves, Opportunities and Promises" provided by Simon, focusing especially on Fermat's law and the electromagnetic waves paper.

- Add a new column to the reading log for Harvard referencing style citations for each source.

- Fill in and complete Form 4 (including aims, objectives, activities and timescales, resources, contingencies, and review points) after the meeting.

- Begin writing the introduction section of the dissertation (targeting about 500 words as a draft), including background information, research topic importance, definitions of keywords/concepts, and start a bibliography section using a consistent referencing style.

- If encountering academic papers that require access, copy the title and author (or link) and send to Simon via group chat for sourcing.
Schedule and organise the remaining 4 hours of academic writing, reflection, and presentation skills sessions with the delegation.

Next SA meeting scheduled for 29th May, 12 PM.
sessions.transcript:
Research Project Progress Review
Simon and Eileen reviewed Eileen's progress on her research project. Eileen had completed form two and started a reading log containing textbooks, lecture notes, videos, and online courses. Simon praised the selection but suggested adding peer-reviewed published literature to strengthen the research foundation. He explained the difference between textbooks (written for pedagogy) and published papers (showing research outcomes), and mentioned finding relevant papers for Eileen to review.

Academic Paper Recommendation Discussion
Simon shared an academic paper titled "Capillary Functions Meet Electromagnetic Waves, Opportunities and Promises" with Eileen, recommending it as a promising resource for her work. He advised Eileen on how to read academic papers effectively and suggested she add this article and others to her reading list, with a particular focus on the paper about Fermat's law and electromagnetic waves. Simon also recommended adding a Harvard referencing column to Eileen's reading list to help with bibliography management when writing essays.

Fermat's Principle Research Discussion
Eileen discussed her research on Fermat's principle and explored potential connections to the brachistochrone problem and catenary curves. Simon suggested that Eileen could potentially include these additional topics in her research, as they all relate to the principle of least action. Simon also mentioned that Eileen's research question could still be modified before completing Form 5, and instructed Eileen to fill out Form 4 after the meeting.

Form 4 Planning and Objectives
Simon explained the importance of Form 4, which includes aims, objectives, activities, and time scales, emphasising the need to demonstrate effective time management and the use of specific verbs in objectives. He instructed Eileen to plan detailed activities, including the completion of the dissertation introduction by mid-May and a presentation, and to identify resources used for the project. Simon stressed the significance of following the SMART principle when setting objectives and ensuring they are achievable within the given timeframe.

EPQ Project Planning Guidance
Simon guided Eileen through the process of planning her EPQ project, focusing on completing Form 4, which includes research background, questions, contingencies, and aims. They discussed the three review points: initial review (before the next meeting), midpoint review (Form 5), and final review (Form 7). Simon also advised Eileen to continue updating Form 6 and to start working on the introduction section of her dissertation, noting that she already had sufficient materials to begin writing.

Dissertation Writing Guidance Session
Simon advised Eileen on writing her dissertation, suggesting she include background information, definitions of keywords, and concepts. He recommended starting with a bibliography section and using a consistent referencing style. Simon also provided guidance on the length of the introduction, suggesting it should be around 500 words in a 5,000-word dissertation, with the main body accounting for 3,500 words.
Google Scholar Research Planning Meeting
Simon and Eileen discussed using Google Scholar for research, with Simon instructing Eileen to explore research papers and take notes for their next discussion. They agreed on May 29th as the date for their next meeting, allowing Eileen time to complete Form 4 and write an introduction before then.

Academic Session Planning Discussion
Simon and Eileen discussed organising the remaining 4 hours of academic sessions, which could be split into two 2-hour sessions or four 1-hour sessions. Simon offered to share relevant papers through the group chat if Eileen needed them, and wished Eileen good luck with her exams.
sessions.generatedReport:
📋 SA Meeting · 2026-04-20  
👤 学生：高菁晗（Eileen）｜ 📖 课题：光与悬链线是否遵循自然界中同一核心的最小作用量原理？  
⏱ 本次课时：40分钟 ｜ 📊 剩余：12 / 15 SA课时  

📝 本次课程概要  
本次学术督导课聚焦于Eileen EPQ项目的阶段性复盘与后续规划。导师与Eileen共同回顾了已完成的Form 2及结构化阅读日志（涵盖教材、视频与在线资源），并就如何提升学术严谨性展开指导。重点讨论了引入同行评议学术论文的必要性，特别推荐了题为《Capillary Functions Meet Electromagnetic Waves, Opportunities and Promises》的核心文献，并围绕费马原理、最速降线与悬链线的物理关联性，探讨了课题深化的可行性。最后，明确了Form 4填写规范与论文引言章节的写作框架与目标要求。

✨ 学生表现与进展  
Eileen展现出扎实的自主学习能力与清晰的逻辑梳理意识——已高质量完成Form 2，并主动构建多源阅读日志；在讨论费马原理与最小作用量的跨领域联系时，提问精准、思考深入，体现出良好的物理直觉与学术好奇心。对导师提出的文献升级建议反应积极，能快速理解教材与学术论文在研究支撑力上的差异，进步显著且持续稳定。

📋 课后任务  
• 精读导师提供的学术论文《Capillary Functions Meet Electromagnetic Waves, Opportunities and Promises》，重点关注其中关于费马定律与电磁波的论述，并将该文及后续新增文献统一补充至阅读日志；  
• 在现有阅读日志中新增一列，按Harvard格式为每项资料规范标注引用信息（作者、年份、标题、来源等）；  
• 完整填写Form 4：包含具体可行的研究目标（建议采用SMART原则）、分阶段活动计划与时间表、所需资源清单、风险应对预案（contingencies）及三个正式审查节点（initial/midpoint/final review）的安排；  
• 启动论文引言撰写（初稿约500字），内容需涵盖研究背景、课题科学意义、核心概念定义（如“最小作用量”“费马原理”“悬链线”等），并同步建立统一格式的参考文献目录；  
• 若在查阅学术论文时遇到访问权限问题，请及时将文献标题、作者或DOI链接发送至本群，导师将协助获取全文。

📅 下次SA Meeting  
✅ 已确定：2026年5月29日（星期四）12:00  
（温馨提示：当前剩余12节SA课时，建议尽早统筹后续学术写作、反思与展示技能训练的4小时专项安排）

💬 导师寄语  
你正在用严谨的思维触碰物理学最优雅的统一性——保持这份专注与热忱，每一个脚注、每一行推导，都在为思想的深度奠基。
